name: Wanted Status Consequence Chain
summary: Baseline cross-system consequence model for how a recognized wanted condition should propagate once locally confirmed in play.

required_propagation_steps.description: Local law or security actors recognize the wanted party through papers, description, rumor, or active notice.
Relevant factions adjust posture: Roadwardens tighten scrutiny, ORE intensifies questioning, Union actors become cautious, criminal brokers raise price or demand leverage.
Checkpoints, escort demands, route denials, or increased document checks alter movement.
Rumor and administrative talk move the status beyond the point of first recognition at a speed appropriate to roads, ports, guild nodes, and mobile institutions.
Inns, officials, merchants, and ordinary residents alter hospitality, trust, pricing, or cooperation.

polity_expression_examples.Shelk: Roadwardens and civic halls notice papers, bounty status, decorum irregularities, and market rumor rapidly along protected roads.
polity_expression_examples.Roaz: ORE and checkpoint culture make formal recognition severe and document-centered.
polity_expression_examples.Union: Contract trust and sanction risk mean rumor can become market exclusion quickly.
polity_expression_examples.Sheresh: Recognition matters most when a traveler is judged as creating survival danger rather than abstract criminality alone.

promotion_rule: No specific party wanted state becomes a persistent world delta until at least one local authority or socially meaningful node confirms recognition in-session.
